data.title: Bybit partners with Tether to boost crypto in Brazil. Bybit, 2nd largest exchange, joins Tether - issuer of USD₮ stablecoin.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE, on July 25, 2025, Bybit, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange in terms of trading volume, revealed a significant alliance with Tether. Tether is a prominent company within the digital asset sector and is the issuer of USD₮, a stablecoin widely utilized across the industry. This partnership is anticipated to have a major impact on the cryptocurrency market due to the stature of both companies involved.

data.title: Abbott and Halmi team up for family-friendly entertainment, agreeing to produce more than 20 original series and films in U.S. at Great Point Studios.
data.description: Bill Abbott, CEO of Great American Media, has revealed a new collaboration between former colleagues to boost U.S.-centered film production. The venture involves a 20+ picture deal spanning multiple years, aiming to create original series and films within the country at Great Point Studios. The announcement was made on July 25, 2025, detailing a significant expansion in the industry through this partnership.

data.title: Kafene partners with LendPro to offer lease-to-own solutions, enhancing retailers' credit options.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in New York City, Kafene, a platform specializing in point-of-sale leasing, revealed their collaboration with LendPro. This partnership aims to provide retailers with the ability to offer customers facing credit limitations the opportunity to utilize lease-to-own ("LTO") agreements for more adaptable purchasing solutions. Kafene's integration with LendPro, a prominent financial services company, signifies their commitment to supporting retailers in catering to a wider consumer base. This strategic move showcases Kafene's dedication to enhancing retail experiences and expanding access for customers seeking alternative payment methods.
NVDA reaches new intraday peak at 174.55.

data.title: Centene posts first quarterly loss in 13 years, with $48.74 billion in sales but a $1.26 loss per share compared to prior year. CEO acknowledges challenges.
data.description: Healthcare insurance company Centene Corporation (NYSE: CNC) experienced a steep decline in its stock price after reporting an unexpected second-quarter 2025 adjusted loss of 16 cents per share, a significant turnaround from an adjusted income of $2.42 per share in the previous year’s quarter. This unexpected loss contrasted sharply with analyst estimates of a $1.26 per share profit, marking Centene's first quarterly loss since the second quarter of 2012. Despite the disappointing earnings, Centene demonstrated robust top-line growth, with sales increasing by 22% year-over-year to $48.74 billion, above the expected $44.48 billion. However, the company's revenue growth was overshadowed by substantial rises in medical costs. The CEO, Sarah London, expressed disappointment in the results but stated the company is working urgently to address the challenges. Premium and service revenues for Centene rose by 18% to $42.5 billion from $36.0 billion the previous year, driven by an increase in premium and membership.
Senior executive purchases 5000 company shares.

data.title: Global 3D weaving market to reach $59.7 million by 2030, growing at 12.7% CAGR, from $32.8 million in 2025.
data.description: Delray Beach, Florida – A recent report by MarketsandMarkets™ forecasts that the worldwide 3D weaving market will see significant growth, increasing from USD 32.8 million in 2025 to USD 59.7 million by 2030. The market is projected to expand at a CAGR of 12.7%. Factors influencing this growth include the rise in renewable energy adoption and infrastructure development initiatives.

data.title: Researchers at Children's Hospital of Philadelphia study ways to prevent pediatric vehicular heatstroke deaths in overheated cars.
data.description: In an effort to prevent children from succumbing to overheating in cars, experts suggest that employing a variety of interventions is most effective. Recent reports from Philadelphia on July 25, 2025 reveal that innovative technologies have been created to aid parents and caregivers in combating pediatric vehicular heatstroke (PVH), a tragic situation where a child perishes due to extreme heat within a vehicle. These interventions are crucial as statistics indicate cases of PVH continue to rise, emphasizing the importance of implementing preventive measures to safeguard children.

data.title: Russia reduces interest rates again as economy slows down, with falling inflation leading to relaxation of record borrowing costs.
data.description: A decrease in inflation rates led the central bank in Moscow to decide to further lower the record-high borrowing costs in the country.

data.title: Labcorp reported Q2 2025 EPS of $4.35 (prev. $3.94), sales up 9.5% to $3.53B (prev. $3.485B), raised guidance.
data.description: Labcorp Holdings Inc. (NYSE: LH) exceeded expectations in the second quarter of 2025. They reported adjusted earnings per share of $4.35, a significant increase from the previous year's $3.94. Sales also grew by 9.5% to $3.53 billion, surpassing the anticipated $3.485 billion. LabCorp raised its adjusted earnings guidance for the fiscal year from the previous range of $15.70-$16.40 to $16.05-$16.50, compared to the expected $16.09. The company also adjusted its sales forecast from $13.978 billion-$14.148 billion to $14.08 billion-$14.23 billion, outperforming the projected $13.897 billion. LabCorp anticipates sales growth of 7.5%-8.6% in 2025, up from the previous guidance of 6.7%-8%. The company experienced a robust second quarter with double-digit revenue growth.

data.title: Black-owned Simply Pure Trenton celebrates 1-Year Anniversary with Cannaversary 2025, featuring Faith Evans, Lil Cease, jazz tribute, and community block party.
data.description: Come celebrate the 1-Year Anniversary of Simply Pure Trenton, a leading Black-owned cannabis dispensary in New Jersey, at Cannaversary 2025. This week-long event will take place in Ewing Township, N.J. starting on July 25, 2025. Simply Pure Trenton is one of the first Black-owned dispensaries in the state and is marking this milestone with a series of special activities and promotions. Don't miss this exciting opportunity to support a diverse business in the cannabis industry.

data.title: Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE: EW) reports Q2 earnings of 67 cents per share, beats estimates of 62 cents. Quarterly sales hit $1.532 billion exceeding expectations of $1.488 billion. FY2025 adjusted EPS guidance $2.40-$2.50, raised sales guidance to $5.90 billion-$6.10 billion.
data.description: Medical technology company, Edwards Lifesciences Corporation (NYSE: EW), surpassed expectations in its second-quarter financial report. The company's adjusted earnings were reported at 67 cents per share, outperforming the market consensus of 62 cents per share. In terms of quarterly sales, Edwards Lifesciences achieved $1.532 billion, exceeding the anticipated $1.488 billion. Furthermore, the company confirmed its adjusted EPS guidance for FY2025 range of $2.40 to $2.50, while raising its FY2025 sales forecast from $5.70 billion-$6.10 billion to $5.90 billion-$6.10 billion. Edwards Lifesciences expressed satisfaction with the double-digit sales growth and conveyed confidence in its yearly outlook based on the robust first half performance and positive developments in its portfolio.
